zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Le Gross於1390年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可可洛斯特維其可時，她的父親Oliver Le Gros21歲，母親Elizabeth Tudenham20歲。她於1421年之前與Sir William Yelverton of Rougham結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1439年在英國英格蘭諾福爾可斯洛利享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Le Gross于1390年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可可洛斯特維其可时，她的父亲Oliver Le Gros21岁，母亲Elizabeth Tudenham20岁。她于1421年之前与Sir William Yelverton of Rougham结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1439年在英国英格兰諾福爾可斯洛利享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Gross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a big man, from Middle High German grōz ‘large, corpulent’, German gross. This surname is also established in some other parts of Europe, most notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine). In Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Slovenia, and Croatia it is also found (in Slovenia almost exclusively) in the Slavicized form Gros (see also 3 below). The Jewish name has been Hebraicized as Gadol .

English: descriptive nickname for a big man, from Middle English gros, grosse, groce ’large; heavy’, also meaning ‘simple, plain’, from Old French gros ‘big, fat’ (from Latin grossus ‘thick’), a word of ancient Germanic origin, thus etymologically the same word as in 1 above.
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian, Polish, Croatian or other Slavic   Gros  , itself of German origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Gadol
meanings: Jewish: Hebraicized form of Gross 1, based on Hebrew gadol ‘large’.

variants.name: Grohs
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Detlef, Florian, Gerhard, Hans, Heinz, Helmut, Kurt, Lothar, Otto.
German: variant of   Gross  .
Altered form of German Groh , with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames.

variants.name: Grossmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Erwin, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Franz, Goetz, Gottlieb, Gunter, Heinz, Helmut, Horst.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): descriptive nickname for a large man, from gross ‘large’ (see Gross ) + Mann ‘man’.

variants.name: Grosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Bernd, Eberhard, Erwin, Gerhard, Gerhardt, Hans, Hermann, Ingo, Otto, Reinhold, Uwe.
German and English: variant of   Gross  .

variants.name: Grosser
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Alois, Bernhard, Christoph, Ernst, Gunther, Siegfried, Wolf.
German:
nickname for a big man, from an inflected form of   Gross  .

variants.name: Groos
meanings: German, Dutch, and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Gross  .

variants.name: Groce
meanings: English (Cornwall, of Norman origin): variant of   Gross  .
Altered form of German   Gross  .

variants.name: Grote
meanings: North German: nickname for a large man, from Low German grot(h) ‘large, corpulent’. Compare Gross and Groth .
Dutch: variant of   Groot  , a cognate of 1 above.

English: nickname from Middle English grot ‘groat, a denomination of coin’, from Middle Dutch groot ‘great’, being an elliptical use of the adjective, i.e. ‘thick (coin)’. In English use, from the 14th to 17th centuries, a groat was a silver coin equivalent to four pence.

variants.name: Gros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Camile, Albon, Alcide, Arsene, Chantelle, Clovis, Elphege, Etienne, Francois, Gabrielle.
French: nickname for a large or fat man, from Old French gros ‘big, fat’, from Late Latin grossus, of ancient Germanic origin. Compare Legros .
Jewish (Ashkenazic) and English (Middlesex, of Norman origin): variant of   Gross  .

variants.name: Grossett
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname from a diminutive or pet form of Middle English gros ‘big; heavy’, also used to mean ‘plain and simple’ (Old French gros ‘big, fat’; see Gross ). This surname is frequent in Jamaica.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Le Gross was born in 1390, in Crostwick, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Oliver Le Gros, was 21 and her mother, Elizabeth Tudenham, was 20. She married Sir William Yelverton of Rougham before 1421. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1439, in Sloley, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Le Gross est née en 1390, à Crostwick, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Oliver Le Gros, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Tudenham, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Yelverton of Rougham avant 1421. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1439, à Sloley, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Le Gross 1390 in Crostwick, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Oliver Le Gros 21 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Tudenham 20. Sie hat vor 1421 Sir William Yelverton of Rougham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1439 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Sloley, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Le Gross è nata il nel 1390 nel Crostwick, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Oliver Le Gros, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Tudenham, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Yelverton of Rougham prima del 1421. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1439, nel Sloley, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Le Gross(이)가 1390년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 크로스트윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Oliver Le Gros(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Tudenham(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1421년 이전 Sir William Yelverton of Rougham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1439년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 슬로리에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Le Gross родилась в 1390 г. в местности кроствикк, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Oliver Le Gros было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Tudenham, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Yelverton of Rougham перед 1421 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1439 г. в местности слоли, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.
